items.title: German conservatives win election, far right in second | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Germany's opposition conservatives won the national election, putting Friedrich Merz on track to be the next chancellor while the far-right AfD came in second, its best-ever result, exit polls showed. 

#News #Germany #Europe #Election #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: Hamas frees six hostages in exchange for Palestinian detainees | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.title: Ukrainian villagers visit ruined homes in Russian-controlled Donetsk | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.title: Trump fires top US general in unprecedented Pentagon shakeup | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>President Trump fired Air Force General C.Q. Brown and said he would nominate former Lieutenant General Dan ‘Razin’ Caine to succeed Brown. It is the first time someone has been pulled out of retirement to become the top military officer.

items.title: Saudi Arabia's role in global diplomacy
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>This week saw US and Russian officials meet in Riyadh to discuss ending the Ukraine war as President Donald Trump attended an investor conference in Miami for Saudi's sovereign wealth fund. It appears Saudi Arabia is flexing its diplomatic muscles. On this weekend podcast, our journalists covering the Gulf, foreign policy and oil discuss how the kingdom and Crown Prince got to this point. 










Sign up for the Reuters Econ World newsletter here (https://www.reuters.com/newsletters/reuters-econ-world/) .






Listen to the Reuters Econ World podcast here (https://nam02.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Flink.chtbl.com%2FAbxd8GDx&data=05%7C02%7CGayle.Issa%40thomsonreuters.com%7C22043c115f5c46b09f9c08dcf3464d32%7C62ccb8646a1a4b5d8e1c397dec1a8258%7C0%7C0%7C638652730474292284%7CUnknown%7CTWFpbGZsb3d8eyJWIjoiMC4wLjAwMDAiLCJQIjoiV2luMzIiLCJBTiI6Ik1haWwiLCJXVCI6Mn0%3D%7C0%7C%7C%7C&sdata=jtNN%2BMKMQ3W6WrxdwmC7fTor0g1hgnW5mgoS%2FBJGPDE%3D&reserved=0) .

items.title: Dancing in Damascus: Syrians cling to culture under Islamist rule | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>On a wintry night in Damascus, hundreds of people packed into a courtyard in the Old City, dancing and singing during a joyful evening of music - with the approval of Syria's new, Islamist-led authorities.

items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.title: Trump, Maine governor clash over transgender athletes
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump clashed with Maine Governor Janet Mills over federal funds the president threatened to withhold from the state over transgender athletes participating in girls and women's sports.
 
Trump was addressing Democratic and Republican governors in the State Dining Room at the White House on Friday when he mentioned his recent executive order banning trans athletes from playing women's sports. He then called on Maine's governor and asked her if the state will comply, prompting Mills to object.
 
🔗 Read about how many athletes openly identify as trans at the collegiate level at our link in bio.
 
#transathletes #tranrights #LGBTQ+ #DonaldTrump #JanetMills #NCAA

Read the story here:

items.title: LIVE: Los Angeles attorney on Menendez brothers case
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman gives an update on the case of Lyle and Erik Menendez, who were convicted in 1996 of first-degree murder and sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole for killing their parents. A recent Netflix documentary series raised new evidence and revived public interest in the case.

#LosAngeles #Menendez #MenendezBrothers #ErikMenendez #LyleMenendez #Legal #Crime #NathanHochman #Reuters #News #Live 

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>
